items.model_answer: Overfitting happens when a model memorizes training data and performs worse on new examples.
items.reference_answer: Overfitting means a model fits training data too closely and does not generalize well.

items.model_answer: Underfitting is when a model is too simple to capture important patterns in the data.
items.reference_answer: Underfitting means a model is too simple and performs poorly on both training and test data.

items.model_answer: Precision measures how many predicted positive cases were actually positive.
items.reference_answer: Precision is true positives divided by all predicted positives.

criteria.name: correctness
criteria.description: Does the answer explain the concept accurately?

criteria.name: clarity
criteria.description: Is the answer easy to understand?

criteria.name: completeness
criteria.description: Does the answer cover the essential points?
